skill.name: website-to-hyperframes
skill.description: Capture a website and create a HyperFrames video from it. Use when: (1) a user provides a URL and wants a video, (2) someone says "capture this site", "turn this into a video", "make a promo from my site", (3) the user wants a social ad, product tour, or any video based on an existing website, (4) the user shares a link and asks for any kind of video content. Even if the user just pastes a URL — this is the skill to use.
skill.summary: Capture any website and produce a professional video with narration, animations, and brand-consistent styling.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Security evaluation confirms this skill is safe for publication. Static analysis flagged 409 potential issues, but all high-severity findings are false positives. The skill uses legitimate HyperFrames CLI tooling (npx hyperframes capture/lint/validate) for video production workflows. External commands are standard CLI tooling, network access is limited to website capture, and env_access is optional API key configuration. No malicious code execution, credential exfiltration, or data harvesting patterns detected.

low_findings.title: External Command Patterns in Documentation
low_findings.description: 287 references to shell commands (npx hyperframes) flagged by static scanner. These are legitimate CLI tooling invocations documented for the video production workflow, not malicious code execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All external_command detections are documentation examples of npx hyperframes CLI calls for capture, lint, and validate operations. No actual shell execution occurs - the skill provides guidance for users to run these tools.

low_findings.title: Optional API Key Environment Access
low_findings.description: References to GEMINI_API_KEY in step-1-capture.md:19. This is an optional, user-provided enhancement for AI-powered image descriptions. Not hardcoded secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly states 'Set GEMINI_API_KEY (or GOOGLE_API_KEY) in a .env file' - indicating user-provided configuration, not hardcoded credentials. No evidence of credential exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Flags
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at multiple locations. Manual review confirms these are false positives triggered by documentation content, not actual cryptographic implementations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill is a video production authoring tool, not a crypto library. No cryptographic operations are performed. Scanner likely triggered on words like 'capture', 'token', or code block contexts.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Screen Capture Upload Flags
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged 'Screen capture upload' as HIGH blocker. This IS the core purpose of the skill - capturing websites to extract design tokens, screenshots, and assets for video production.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Website capture is the explicit, intended function of this skill. The captured content (screenshots, fonts, assets) is used locally to produce videos - no unauthorized upload to external servers.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at 287 locations. These are markdown code blocks containing shell command examples, not actual backtick execution patterns.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Scanner misinterpreted markdown code fences (```bash) as backtick execution. The skill contains documentation examples, not actual executable code. No eval(), exec(), or dynamic command construction present.

content.user_title: Create professional videos from any website
content.value_statement: Converting website designs into engaging videos requires complex technical steps from capture to render. This skill automates the entire 7-step pipeline, producing professional videos with narration, animations, and brand-consistent styling from a simple URL.
actual_capabilities: Capture any website URL and extract design tokens, fonts, screenshots, and assets
Generate professional narration scripts with specified timing and beats
Create per-beat storyboards with mood, camera, animations, and transitions
Build animated HTML compositions using GSAP animations and captured assets
Generate TTS audio with word-level timestamps synced to storyboard beats
Render final video via HyperFrames capture engine with optional MP4 export
limitations: Requires Claude Code or compatible AI assistant with file system access
Website capture quality depends on target site complexity and JavaScript rendering
MP4 rendering requires Docker environment with Chrome and FFmpeg

use_cases.title: Product launch videos
use_cases.description: Capture a product website and create a polished launch video with animations, logo reveals, and product feature highlights.

use_cases.title: Social media ads
use_cases.description: Transform any website into Instagram or TikTok ads with kinetic typography, brand colors, and optional voiceover.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Website portfolio videos
use_cases.description: Create video walkthroughs of portfolio sites for use in presentations, pitches, or social proof.

prompt_templates.title: Simple website capture
prompt_templates.prompt: Capture https://example.com and make me a 25-second product launch video
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - basic video from single URL

prompt_templates.title: Social ad with specific format
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this website into a 15-second social ad for Instagram: https://mysite.com

prompt_templates.title: Product demo with voiceover
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30-second product demo video from https://product.com using the default voiceover voice

prompt_templates.title: Full production with custom timing
prompt_templates.prompt: Capture https://landingpage.io and produce a 45-second brand reel with: 3 intro beats, 2 feature highlights, 1 CTA. Use portrait format 1080x1920.

output_examples.input: Capture https://stripe.com and make me a 20-second product demo
output_examples.output: A complete HyperFrames project with: capture/ folder (screenshots, assets, tokens), DESIGN.md, SCRIPT.md, STORYBOARD.md, compositions/ with animated HTML, narration.wav, transcript.json, and localhost Studio preview URL.

output_examples.input: Turn this website into a social ad: https://example.com
output_examples.output: A 15-second Instagram Stories video (1080x1920) with brand-consistent animations, optional hook narration, and smooth transitions between beats.

best_practices: Start with websites that have clear visual hierarchy and distinct sections for best capture results
Review captured assets and design tokens before writing the script to ensure brand consistency
Use the validation step (Step 7) to catch layout issues before rendering to MP4
anti_patterns: Do not skip the capture step and provide raw URLs to sub-agents - always capture first
Do not inline assets (SVG/image data) in compositions - always reference captured files by path
Do not use Google Fonts when captured fonts are available - use @font-face with local woff2 files

faq.question: What websites work best with this skill?
faq.answer: Static marketing sites, landing pages, and product pages work best. Sites with heavy JavaScript interactions or login walls may have limited capture quality.

faq.question: Do I need any API keys?
faq.answer: No API keys are required for basic capture and video production. Optionally, you can set GEMINI_API_KEY for AI-powered image descriptions (~$0.001/image).

faq.question: What video formats are supported?
faq.answer: Landscape (1920x1080), Portrait (1080x1920 for Instagram Stories/TikTok), and Square (1080x1080 for Instagram feed).

faq.question: Can I add my own narration?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill generates TTS, but you can replace narration.wav with your own voiceover recording. Update the transcript.json with your word-level timestamps.

faq.question: How long does rendering take?
faq.answer: Capture takes 10-30 seconds. Building compositions takes 2-5 minutes depending on complexity. Final MP4 rendering via Docker takes 1-3 minutes.

faq.question: What's the difference between the preview URL and the MP4 file?
faq.answer: The localhost Studio URL (http://localhost:port) is the live preview - share this first for review. The MP4 file is the final deliverable - render on explicit request only.
